metrics.narrative: The buyer's stated objectives are directional rather than quantified: on the 8 Oct 2025 call the buyer's CPO (Jihad Ibrahim) named 'efficiency of our work' and reducing 'the total cost of ownership' via AI-integrated tools as strategic goals, and asked for budgetary figures to size the cost. On 30 Mar 2026 the Zycus rep built a draft ROI calculator to quantify this business case with Jihad Ibrahim for a CFO presentation, but there is no confirmation the CFO review happened or that a quantified number exists today.

champion.narrative: Jihad Ibrahim is the most senior procurement professional engaged and has driven the evaluation since Sep 2025 (Abu Dhabi World Tour, per Next Step 9 Sep 2025), including an onsite meeting in Abu Dhabi on 30 Oct 2025. The Zycus rep explicitly noted on 23 Jan 2026 wanting to 'turn Jihad into a champion' by offering a live demo and case studies and stressing a regular communication cadence — language that concedes he is not yet a confirmed champion. His responsiveness has been uneven (a roughly two-month silence from 23 Jan 2026 to 13 Mar 2026 while budget approval was pending internally), and there is no evidence he has surfaced Zycus favorably to the CFO or other executives.

competition.narrative: Coupa and SAP Ariba were named by the buyer's CPO (Jihad Ibrahim) as the shortlisted incumbents as early as 9 Sep 2025, and on the 8 Oct 2025 call a second buyer attendee asked directly which of the two Zycus is closer to and whether all competitor features are matched. The Zycus rep positioned Zycus as closer to Coupa on UI/P2P strength while ahead on AI maturity (Generative AI Studio since 2018, '7-8 years ahead'), and the CRM also cites a 2026 Gartner Leader recognition shared with the buyer. All three vendors remain live in the same formal RFP process submitted 10 Jun 2026, with no evaluation feedback yet.

identify_pain.narrative: The buyer's CPO (Jihad Ibrahim) articulated the pain directly on the 8 Oct 2025 call: fragmented procurement across a multi-entity group, a desire to reduce total cost of ownership, and interest in AI-integrated tools to drive efficiency. The CRM's MEDDPICC note (15 May 2026) infers this reflects a lack of centralised procurement platform and group-level spend visibility, consistent with the group-wide framing Jihad Ibrahim gave.

paper_process.narrative: No MSA, SOW, or contracting mechanics have been discussed anywhere in the record — appropriate for a deal still in the RFP round of Formal Evaluation, but this means the entire post-selection paper trail (commercials lock, legal, InfoSec, signature, PO) has not yet started, which is materially at odds with a 31 Jul 2026 close date.

economic_buyer.narrative: Jihad Ibrahim is the decision maker running the evaluation, but per the CRM's own MEDDPICC 2.0 note (last updated 15 May 2026), he 'will require senior approval (TBC)' — the actual budget owner is unnamed and unengaged. On 25 Mar 2026 Jihad Ibrahim confirmed the deal was 'on hold' while 'securing budget approval', and no subsequent note confirms that approval cleared. No CFO (two CFO-titled account contacts exist, both unengaged) has had any direct Zycus contact in nine-plus months.

decision_process.narrative: The process moved from informal evaluation and pricing exchange (Oct 2025 – Jan 2026) into a formal RFP: RFP received 7 May 2026, clarifications deadline extended to 15 May, response deadline extended twice to 10 Jun 2026, and the response was submitted 10 Jun 2026 with a retained discount held back for negotiation. As of this sweep (~29 days post-submission) no evaluation outcome or scoring feedback has surfaced.

decision_criteria.narrative: On the 8 Oct 2025 call the buyer's CPO (Jihad Ibrahim) laid out clear criteria: group-wide benefit across the holding's companies (not just one entity), efficiency gains, and total-cost-of-ownership reduction; a second attendee separately probed implementation cost ('how much more expensive than the Big Four') and full feature parity versus Coupa and SAP Ariba. These criteria carried through into the formal RFP process (submitted 10 Jun 2026).

flags: Single-threaded risk: Only Jihad Ibrahim engaged across 9-month cycle; Chad Bautista (Executive Sponsor contact role) has never appeared; no CFO, no C-level sponsor, no multi-stakeholder validation. If Jihad leaves, is overruled, or goes silent (currently 16 days no reply), the deal stalls or dies. Multi-thread immediately: secure CFO engagement via ROI co-presentation (Dan's 30 Mar offer still open), add procurement team members from 30 Oct on-site meeting (Arun mentioned but not a contact role), and request introduction to Executive Sponsor or C-level digital/finance lead.

Indefensible close date: 28 days to close 2026-07-31 requires RFP decision + CFO budget sign-off + vendor selection + pricing negotiation + MSA/SOW + InfoSec + ERP scoping. Buyer timeline was 'vague, est 3-6 months' per Next Step 2025-10-30. Close will slip to Q3 or Q4 2026.

Position AI/Merlin as the group-wide efficiency unlock and the wedge against Ariba/Coupa — National Holding's stated criteria are efficiency gains and TCO reduction across the group; Zycus's positioned differentiator is AI maturity ('7-8 years ahead', generative AI since 2018). But the RFP response (submitted 10 Jun 2026) is a black box — there is no confirmation the AI/Merlin narrative landed or that the evaluation committee understands how Zycus's AI capabilities translate to measurable efficiency vs Ariba/Coupa's newer AI plays. The next move is to offer a post-RFP AI deep-dive session (30-45 min) where Dan walks the evaluation committee through 2-3 live Merlin use cases (AI-driven sourcing event setup, contract risk extraction, spend anomaly detection) and quantifies the time savings vs manual workflows. Use the session to reframe the RFP decision as 'AI-mature platform today (Zycus) vs AI catch-up roadmap (Ariba/Coupa)', and anchor the efficiency gains to the group's procurement digitalization strategy (23 Jan 2026 email from Jihad).

Re-land the AI differentiation story (2018 Generative AI Studio, full S2P suite) directly with Jihad Ibrahim in writing now, since it was delivered live on 8 Oct 2025 but has not been reinforced since RFP submission.
best_practice_check.summary: The dominant lever is access to power: nobody who controls the budget has ever been in a room with Zycus. Multi-threading and competitive-intel refresh follow close behind.

customer_preference.evidence: No buyer-voiced preference for Zycus over Coupa or SAP Ariba is on record since the RFP was submitted 10 Jun 2026; the most recent buyer sentiment (8 Oct 2025 call) was general interest, not a stated preference.

competitive_position.summary: Zycus is one of three vendors in a live, parallel RFP evaluation. The competitive shortlist (Coupa, SAP Ariba, Zycus) was set informally as early as 9 Sep 2025 and has carried through into the formal RFP process submitted 10 Jun 2026, but no fresh competitive intelligence (buyer preference, scoring, feedback) has surfaced since the submission.

deal_scores_evidence.summary: A live, single-threaded RFP evaluation: the formal response is in, the buyer is still replying, but the budget owner has never been in the room and the close date is far tighter than the work remaining.

deal_risk.text: Close date 31 Jul 2026 is only 22 days out, but RFP evaluation, budget approval, vendor selection, and contracting are all still outstanding — the date is not realistic.
The economic buyer (the CFO who must give senior approval) is unmapped and has never been directly engaged in over nine months of pursuit.
The deal remains single-threaded on Jihad Ibrahim, with no confirmed second active buyer-side relationship.
Zycus is in an active three-way RFP fight against Coupa and SAP Ariba with no competitive intelligence gathered since the 10 Jun 2026 submission.

win_position.text: The RFP response was formally submitted 10 Jun 2026 with a discount deliberately retained for negotiation, a genuine milestone completed inside the Formal Evaluation round.

No economic buyer has been directly engaged in nine-plus months; the CRM's own MEDDPICC note (updated 15 May 2026) states the decision maker 'will require senior approval (CFO TBC)', which caps how confident a win read can be.

The deal is single-threaded: only Jihad Ibrahim (Group Director of Procurement) has been an evidenced active participant since Sep 2025; the Executive Sponsor contact role (Chad Bautista) was invited to the 30 Oct 2025 onsite but attendance was never confirmed (the meeting was not recorded).

Engagement is current and real: an incoming buyer email reply was logged 3 Jul 2026, six days before this sweep, following a 2 Jul 2026 rep email to arrange a follow-up meeting with Jihad Ibrahim and the deal owner.

No RFP evaluation feedback has surfaced in the roughly 29 days since the 10 Jun 2026 submission, and National Holding is evaluating Zycus in parallel against Coupa and SAP Ariba (shortlisted since 9 Sep 2025) with no recent read on standing.

deal_momentum.text: A real, two-way buyer touch (incoming email reply) landed 3 Jul 2026, six days before this sweep, keeping the deal firmly live.
Cadence has been uneven historically: a roughly two-month silence from 23 Jan 2026 to 13 Mar 2026 while internal budget approval was 'on hold'.
The RFP response was formally submitted 10 Jun 2026, a concrete deliverable landed after two deadline extensions (15 May, then 10 Jun).
No confirmed future meeting date exists; the 2 Jul 2026 rep email is an ask awaiting a reply, not yet a scheduled session.

customer_commitment.text: The buyer has expressed interest in proceeding ('we are interested to move on to the next step', 8 Oct 2025 call) but no recent buyer-voiced preference of Zycus over Coupa or SAP Ariba is on record.

Budget approval has sat with internal management since 23 Jan 2026 ('submitted our procurement strategy to the management for their review and approval') with no confirmation since 25 Mar 2026 that it has cleared.

hard.ais_why:
• On 23 Jan 2026, CPO Jihad wrote to the rep: 'We are in the midst of setting our procurement strategy, with a primary focus on the digitalization and automation of our procurement function. At this stage, we have submitted our procurement strategy to the management for their review and approval.' Buyer framing is 'digitalization and automation' ‚Äî adjacent to AI but not naming AI/Merlin/agentic.
• On 7 May 2026: 'RFP received, under review - clarifications deadline 15th May, Response deadline 20th May.' On 30 Mar 2026: 'to help him build out budget approval I have built a quick ROI calculator and shared with him a draft output, suggesting we build it out together and co-present to the CFO.' Jihad is engaging deeply but the conversation is digitalization/ROI focused. ANA is in product scope (only AI SKU); Merlin/Merlin Intake/Agentic AI are absent.
• Confidence is Medium. National Holding has a real RFP in flight (received 7 May), an engaged CPO (Jihad), and a stated digitalization/automation thesis. But 'digitalization and automation' is process-language, not AI-specific. With only ANA in AI scope and no explicit AI demand from buyer, the score sits at 5 (boundary DOWN per rubric).
